# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

# This is an example of a Deployment Manager template that deploys the complete GCP application stack for your project including Compute Engine, 
# SQL instance (Cloud SQL), Storage and Load Balancer with basic configurations to suit MVP deployment. Skips Cloud Logging & Monitoring configuration as per requirement focus on core infrastructure only.  
resources:
- name: projects/mygcloudprojectid/bucketstoragesettings/{storage}  # replace this part according your project id and bucket setups you want to use in the future (you can change it later) - no spaces allowed here, must be lower case without any special characters. e.g., example
  type: storage.v1.bucketSasettingName   # This line replaces {storage} with your unique Bucket name for this project and globally uniq in GCP world which is the standard naming convention due to its uniqueness across all projects, must be lower case without any special characters
  properties:   
      bucketConfigs:[{"name":"exampleBucket",location:"US","storageClass": "STANDARD"}] # replace example with a unique name for your Bucket - no spaces allowed here. Must have exactly one object and its metadata names must match the following schema to avoid conflicts (must be lower case without any special characters).
- name: projects/mygcloudprojectid/{instance}/instancesettings 
  type: compute.v1.instanceSettingsName   # This line replaces {instance} with your unique Instance Name - no spaces allowed here, must have exactly one object and its metadata names match the following schema to avoid conflicts (must be lower case without any special characters).    
- name: projects/mygcloudprojectid/{loadbalancer}/networksettings   # replace this part according project id  that you want use in future. no spaces allowed here, must have exactly one object and its metadata names match the following schema to avoid conflicts (must be lower case without any special characters).
